subtitle: Know where every dollar goes, every month

views.name: DashboardView
views.purpose: Command center showing total monthly burn rate in a large hero number, a donut chart of spending by category, upcoming renewals within 7 days as an alert list, and a quick-add FAB. Tapping the hero number toggles between monthly and yearly view.

views.name: EntrySheet
views.purpose: Add or edit a subscription with service name, amount, billing cycle picker, category picker, next renewal date, usage rating slider (1-5 stars with emoji labels from 'Never' to 'Daily'), active toggle, and optional notes. Smart defaults: monthly cycle, today+30 for renewal, active=true.

views.name: HistoryView
views.purpose: Full subscription list grouped by category with active/cancelled segment filter. Each row shows service name, normalized monthly cost, next renewal date, usage rating dots, and billing cycle badge. Swipe to cancel (sets isActive=false and records cancellation). Sort by cost, name, or renewal date.

views.name: AnalyticsView
views.purpose: Multi-section analytics: (1) Monthly spending trend line chart over past 12 months, (2) Category breakdown horizontal bar chart ranked by spend, (3) Cost vs Usage scatter insight highlighting wasteful subscriptions (high cost + low usage), (4) Savings summary from cancelled subscriptions, (5) AI-style insights like 'You spend more on Entertainment than 3 other categories combined'

viewModelComputedProperties.name: totalMonthlyCost
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Sum of all active subscriptions normalized to monthly cost. Weekly×4.33, quarterly÷3, semiAnnual÷6, yearly÷12.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: totalYearlyCost
viewModelComputedProperties.description: totalMonthlyCost × 12 — the annualized burn rate for all active subscriptions.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: dailyCostRate
viewModelComputedProperties.description: totalMonthlyCost / 30.0 — how much subscriptions cost per day, shown as 'You spend $X.XX/day on subscriptions'.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: activeSubscriptionCount
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Count of entries where isActive == true.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: cancelledSubscriptionCount
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Count of entries where isActive == false.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: upcomingRenewals
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Active subscriptions with nextRenewalDate within the next 7 days, sorted by date ascending. Powers the dashboard alert list.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: categoryBreakdown
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Active subscriptions grouped by category with normalized monthly totals and percentage of total spend. Sorted descending by monthlyTotal. Powers the donut chart and bar chart.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: mostExpensiveCategory
viewModelComputedProperties.description: The category with the highest normalized monthly spend across all active subscriptions.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: mostExpensiveSubscription
viewModelComputedProperties.description: The single active subscription with the highest normalized monthly cost.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: lowUsageHighCost
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Active subscriptions where usageRating <= 2 AND normalized monthly cost is above average. These are 'waste candidates' — subscriptions costing more than average that the user barely uses. Powers the key insight card.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: monthlySpendingHistory
viewModelComputedProperties.description: For the past 12 months, reconstructs what the monthly total would have been based on subscription start dates and cancellation dates. Powers the trend line chart.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: savingsFromCancelled
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Sum of normalized monthly costs of all cancelled (isActive=false) subscriptions — represents monthly savings achieved by cancelling.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: averageCostPerSubscription
viewModelComputedProperties.description: totalMonthlyCost / activeSubscriptionCount — the average monthly cost per active subscription.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: currentStreak
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Consecutive months the user has stayed at or under their monthly budget goal. Resets to 0 if any month exceeds the budget.

viewModelComputedProperties.name: budgetProgress
viewModelComputedProperties.description: totalMonthlyCost / monthlyBudgetGoal — progress toward budget ceiling. Values > 1.0 mean over budget. Powers the progress ring (inverted: lower is better).

viewModelComputedProperties.name: insights
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Dynamic array of human-readable insight strings generated from data analysis. Examples: 'Entertainment costs more than Productivity, Fitness, and Education combined', 'You could save $14.99/mo by cancelling low-usage subscriptions', 'Your subscription costs increased 23% in the last 3 months', '3 subscriptions renew this week totaling $45.97'.

dailyGoal.description: Monthly subscription budget ceiling. The goal is to stay UNDER this amount. Progress ring fills as spending approaches the limit — green when under 70%, yellow 70-90%, red above 90%.

domainLogic.streakDefinition: A streak month is counted when the total normalized monthly subscription cost stays at or below the user's monthly budget goal for the entire calendar month. The streak resets when any month exceeds the budget. This encourages regular subscription auditing and cancellation of unused services.
domainLogic.trendAnalysis: Track month-over-month change in total subscription cost. Compare current month total vs 3-month rolling average to detect subscription creep (gradual increase from free trials converting, price increases, or forgotten signups). Flag when monthly cost increases by more than 10% compared to 3-month average.
insights: Waste detection: Flag active subscriptions with usageRating ≤ 2 and above-average cost as cancellation candidates with exact monthly savings
Category dominance: Identify when a single category exceeds 40% of total spend and compare against others
Subscription creep alert: Detect when total monthly cost has increased more than 15% over the past 3 months
Renewal clustering: Warn when 3+ subscriptions renew within the same 7-day window (cash flow impact)
Annual vs monthly savings: For monthly-billed subscriptions, calculate potential savings if switched to yearly billing (assume 16% discount)
Price per day: Show cost-per-day for each subscription to reframe perceived value (e.g., 'Netflix costs you $0.50/day')
Milestone celebration: Acknowledge when user cancels a subscription ('You just saved $X/year!') or when savings from cancelled subs exceed a threshold
Budget proximity: Alert when within 90% of monthly budget ceiling with specific amount remaining
